title: Chaya Someswara Swamy temple
tagline: Chaya Someswara Swamy temple — spiritual town of Telangana

overview.short: Chaya Someshwara Temple is a Shaivite Hindu temple located in Panagal, Nalgonda district of Telangana, India.
overview.description: Chaya Someshwara Temple is a Shaivite Hindu temple located in Panagal, Nalgonda district of Telangana, India. It was built around the mid-11th century during the rule of the Kunduru Chodas (a branch of Telugu Cholas), supported and embellished further by later Hindu dynasties of Telangana. Some date it to late 11th to early 12th-century. The temple, now partially restored, was in ruins in mid 20th-century.

faq.q: What is the best time to visit Chaya Someswara Swamy temple?
faq.a: The best time to visit Chaya Someswara Swamy temple is Oct – Mar. Summers are 26–40°C and winters are 12–28°C.

faq.q: How do I reach Chaya Someswara Swamy temple?
faq.a: The nearest airport is Rajiv Gandhi International Airport, Hyderabad (94 km) and the nearest railway station is Kacheguda (Hyderabad) (92 km). Roads are generally good; check seasonal/monsoon conditions before high-altitude or remote stretches.

faq.q: How many days are enough for Chaya Someswara Swamy temple?
faq.a: 3 days are usually enough to cover the top attractions of Chaya Someswara Swamy temple at a relaxed pace.

faq.q: How much does a hotel cost in Chaya Someswara Swamy temple?
faq.a: Stays in Chaya Someswara Swamy temple start from ₹900 per night, with options across 4 price categories.

faq.q: What are the top places to visit in Chaya Someswara Swamy temple?
faq.a: The top attractions are Pachala Someswara Temple, Velmakanne, Chakirala, Tripuraram, Chandampally.
